steps.title: Use the triangle sum
steps.content:
The angles in a triangle add to $180^\circ$. Substitute the given measures for angles $P$ and $Q$.
$$104^\circ+38^\circ+R=180^\circ$$

steps.title: Solve for angle R
steps.content:
Subtract the known angles from $180^\circ$.
$$R=180^\circ-104^\circ-38^\circ=38^\circ$$
This also fits the condition that two angles have the same measure. The answer is A.
